spec: CASC-0' harness RunSpec — the STATIC-CASE / M2-isolator factorial (size x medium x verifier) from docs/next/arch/cascade-synthesis.md section 3, developed for registry/experiments/casc-0.json (DRAFT at authoring; the frozen record, once frozen, is authoritative for every design constant restated here). This manifest pins harness INPUTS only; it may not change any design field or threshold. PINNING DIRECTION: the record's pins.harness_manifest is a declared PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholder filled by ops amendment with the staged-bytes manifest sha printed by poc/casc-0/modal/modal_casc0.py BEFORE any final-phase run; this file must NOT contain that sha (circular).

pins.dcasc0ManifestNote: file-level sha256s of the committed d-casc0 bytes (builder data/d-casc0/build-dcasc0.py, generator seed dcasc0/1|casc-0|20260711, single-draw rule); the corpus-level kot-corpus-hash/1 digest is pinned in the registry record pins.corpus_hashes and the runner recomputes BOTH levels fail-closed (ERR_PIN)
pins.kernelPinsNote: kernel-v0 / molecules-v0 pinned VERBATIM from registry/experiments/f2b-transfer.json pins.corpus_hashes — the kernel instance whose AUTHORED content the kernel-gloss (raw-kernel Option-A) medium's authoring style naturalises; the d-casc0 relation records themselves are SELF-AUTHORED in the casc0: namespace (rider); the ASM-1158 pre-freeze explicator check was EXECUTED 2026-07-11 (poc/casc-0/kernel-coverage.md): mapping FAILED (0/7 core relation types kernel-record-backed in the relational sense; only the gendered surfaces mother/father map, to urn:molecule-v0:mother / urn:molecule-v0:father), so the rider clause is 'kernel-STYLE', never 'kernel-covered'; kernel-v0/molecules-v0 stay pinned as the gloss medium's authoring-STYLE anchor only

R2.pinned_by: same revision as the a5-llm R2 pin (docs/design-a5-llm.md); in-programme R-2 rung anchor (programme section 4)

R3.pinned_by: VERBATIM reuse of registry/experiments/f2b-transfer.json pins.model_revisions (same revision as f2b-replicate; backend Modal-smoked there)

models.tuning: ALL CELLS STOCK — no fine-tuning on any side (PROPOSED-ASM-1150; ASM-0852 tuning symmetry satisfied trivially at T_k=0); if tuning is ever added the run is re-labelled an informative screen in the prereg itself (cascade-synthesis section 3, adopted verbatim)

modal_image.note: REUSE of the pinned f2b/f2b-transfer Modal image (PROPOSED-ASM-1153): same requirements-image.txt bytes, no new dependency, no new image build intended; the modal wrapper rebuilds from the identical pinned requirements so the digest recorded in provenance must be compared to this pin at collection

eval_set.rule: EVAL SET = the first per_arm_items (300) items of data/d-casc0/items/eval.jsonl in pinned rank order (rank-interleaved depths, so the prefix keeps the 100/150/150 depth mix at 75/113/112); deterministic prefix, no sampling; floor eval_floor (250), below which the runner refuses (ERR_EVAL_FLOOR)
eval_set.scoring: item_correct = final constrained answer key == the item's ENGINE-derived gold key; gold is engine-derived by construction (SELF-AUTHORED / kernel-STYLE / engine-derived-gold rider on every verdict sentence — 'kernel-STYLE' per the executed ASM-1158 mapping, poc/casc-0/kernel-coverage.md; NO external adjudication in this probe — PROPOSED-ASM-1157); the runner RE-DERIVES every item's cumulative relation states from facts + composition-table and fail-closes on any mismatch with the committed states_core (ERR_ENGINE_GOLD)

design_constants_from_registry_record.note: copied from registry/experiments/casc-0.json (DRAFT) for runner convenience; the record is authoritative once frozen
design_constants_from_registry_record.verifier_levels_note: verifier on ONLY for structured mediums (gloss, plain) — NL has no IR-hard islands (cascade-synthesis section 3, PROPOSED-ASM-1145); verifier checks INTERMEDIATE cumulative relations only, NEVER the final answer (anti-oracle rule, PROPOSED-ASM-1144)

engagement_gate.note: decidable = items with >=1 intermediate hop (depth>=3; 225/300 = 0.75 by the prefix mix); RT-7a made structural, INSTRUMENT-INVALID on failure; the runner only EMITS the block

ttc.reference_cell: R2/gloss/verifier-on (fixed pre-registered reference — the raw-kernel Option-A medium, the maintainer's primary; never post-hoc 'best')
ttc.protocol: self-consistency majority vote over N fresh samples at temperature 1.0, N = clamp(round(ref_flops_per_item / nl_flops_per_item), n_min, n_max) from the SAME run's measured cells; ties broken by pinned option order; /gates/ttc_match_valid requires |flops(ttc cell) - flops(ref cell)| / flops(ref cell) <= match_band_rel

deranged_control.algorithm: Sattolo cyclic permutation over the sorted 3 ATOMIC relation types via random.Random(perm_seed) — a derangement by construction; every store fact's rel_core is mapped through it before the verifier's closure is derived; the realized map + sha are written to deranged-map.json and emitted on every deranged-control record
deranged_control.note: house control on ONE verifier-on cell (R2/gloss/verifier-on topology): identical retry topology and cost, closure content decoupled from items; expected collapse of the verifier benefit; REPORTED-ONLY, never verdict-bearing (PROPOSED-ASM-1152)

arms.factorial: 2 sizes x 3 mediums verifier-off + 2 sizes x 2 structured mediums verifier-on = 10 cells x 3 seeds
arms.ttc-deflator: R2/nl + self-consistency TTC cost-matched to the fixed reference cell (K2' instrument) x 3 seeds
arms.deranged-control: R2/gloss/verifier-on with the deranged closure x 3 seeds

arm_plan.protocol: IDENTICAL elicitation protocol in every cell: for each item, the model asserts each intermediate cumulative relation rel(X0,Xt), t=2..depth-1, as a constrained choice over the item's 7-option gendered label set, each asserted claim is appended to the context in the CELL'S OWN medium rendering, then the final relation is a constrained choice over the same option set; cells differ ONLY in (model size, medium rendering, verifier on/off). Verifier-on: each asserted intermediate is checked against the engine closure (surface -> core via the subject's gender, compared to the derived state); ANY rejected intermediate => reject attempt => resample the whole item (fresh per-attempt sampling), max retry_budget retries; final answer = last attempt (pre-declared, no post-hoc pick); the FINAL relation is NEVER checked (anti-oracle)
arm_plan.attempt0_decoding: argmax over option sequence-logprobs; retries and TTC samples: seeded multinomial over option softmax (carried from f2b HFLM.choose)
arm_plan.extraction_note: P10-style extraction gate NOT APPLICABLE: answers are constrained selections over pinned option keys, so extraction failure is structurally impossible; the instrument gates here are engagement + headroom + separation + ttc-match (PROPOSED-ASM-1156)

nl.qa_prefix: Read the family facts, then answer the relationship question.
nl.intermediate_question: Based only on the facts above, how is {subj} related to {obj}?
nl.final_question: Based only on the facts above, how is {subj} related to {obj}?

gloss.qa_prefix: Kinship claim records follow, one per line: a typed head (type, surface, agent, patient, direction), then a gloss clause that reads the head in plain words. Answer each relation query from these records.

fact_line.mother: [claim kinship/1] type: casc0:rel/parent | surface: "mother" | agent: {subj} ({subj_urn}) | patient: {obj} ({obj_urn}) | direction: agent-to-patient | gloss: {subj} is a woman; {subj} is a parent of {obj}.
fact_line.father: [claim kinship/1] type: casc0:rel/parent | surface: "father" | agent: {subj} ({subj_urn}) | patient: {obj} ({obj_urn}) | direction: agent-to-patient | gloss: {subj} is a man; {subj} is a parent of {obj}.
fact_line.daughter: [claim kinship/1] type: casc0:rel/child | surface: "daughter" | agent: {subj} ({subj_urn}) | patient: {obj} ({obj_urn}) | direction: agent-to-patient | gloss: {subj} is a child of {obj}; {subj} is female.
fact_line.son: [claim kinship/1] type: casc0:rel/child | surface: "son" | agent: {subj} ({subj_urn}) | patient: {obj} ({obj_urn}) | direction: agent-to-patient | gloss: {subj} is a child of {obj}; {subj} is male.
fact_line.sister: [claim kinship/1] type: casc0:rel/sibling | surface: "sister" | agent: {subj} ({subj_urn}) | patient: {obj} ({obj_urn}) | direction: symmetric | gloss: {subj} and {obj} are siblings; {subj} is female.
fact_line.brother: [claim kinship/1] type: casc0:rel/sibling | surface: "brother" | agent: {subj} ({subj_urn}) | patient: {obj} ({obj_urn}) | direction: symmetric | gloss: {subj} and {obj} are siblings; {subj} is male.

claim_line.mother: [derived kinship/1] status: asserted-by-reasoner | type: casc0:rel/parent | surface: "mother" | agent: {subj} | patient: {obj} | direction: agent-to-patient | gloss: {subj} is a woman; {subj} is a parent of {obj}.
claim_line.father: [derived kinship/1] status: asserted-by-reasoner | type: casc0:rel/parent | surface: "father" | agent: {subj} | patient: {obj} | direction: agent-to-patient | gloss: {subj} is a man; {subj} is a parent of {obj}.
claim_line.daughter: [derived kinship/1] status: asserted-by-reasoner | type: casc0:rel/child | surface: "daughter" | agent: {subj} | patient: {obj} | direction: agent-to-patient | gloss: {subj} is a child of {obj}; {subj} is female.
claim_line.son: [derived kinship/1] status: asserted-by-reasoner | type: casc0:rel/child | surface: "son" | agent: {subj} | patient: {obj} | direction: agent-to-patient | gloss: {subj} is a child of {obj}; {subj} is male.
claim_line.sister: [derived kinship/1] status: asserted-by-reasoner | type: casc0:rel/sibling | surface: "sister" | agent: {subj} | patient: {obj} | direction: symmetric | gloss: {subj} and {obj} are siblings; {subj} is female.
claim_line.brother: [derived kinship/1] status: asserted-by-reasoner | type: casc0:rel/sibling | surface: "brother" | agent: {subj} | patient: {obj} | direction: symmetric | gloss: {subj} and {obj} are siblings; {subj} is male.
claim_line.grandmother: [derived kinship/1] status: asserted-by-reasoner | type: casc0:rel/grandparent | surface: "grandmother" | agent: {subj} | patient: {obj} | direction: agent-to-patient | gloss: {subj} is a woman; {subj} is a grandparent of {obj}.
claim_line.grandfather: [derived kinship/1] status: asserted-by-reasoner | type: casc0:rel/grandparent | surface: "grandfather" | agent: {subj} | patient: {obj} | direction: agent-to-patient | gloss: {subj} is a man; {subj} is a grandparent of {obj}.
claim_line.granddaughter: [derived kinship/1] status: asserted-by-reasoner | type: casc0:rel/grandchild | surface: "granddaughter" | agent: {subj} | patient: {obj} | direction: agent-to-patient | gloss: {subj} is a grandchild of {obj}; {subj} is female.
claim_line.grandson: [derived kinship/1] status: asserted-by-reasoner | type: casc0:rel/grandchild | surface: "grandson" | agent: {subj} | patient: {obj} | direction: agent-to-patient | gloss: {subj} is a grandchild of {obj}; {subj} is male.
claim_line.aunt: [derived kinship/1] status: asserted-by-reasoner | type: casc0:rel/pibling | surface: "aunt" | agent: {subj} | patient: {obj} | direction: agent-to-patient | gloss: {subj} is an aunt of {obj}.
claim_line.uncle: [derived kinship/1] status: asserted-by-reasoner | type: casc0:rel/pibling | surface: "uncle" | agent: {subj} | patient: {obj} | direction: agent-to-patient | gloss: {subj} is an uncle of {obj}.
claim_line.niece: [derived kinship/1] status: asserted-by-reasoner | type: casc0:rel/nibling | surface: "niece" | agent: {subj} | patient: {obj} | direction: agent-to-patient | gloss: {subj} is a niece of {obj}.
claim_line.nephew: [derived kinship/1] status: asserted-by-reasoner | type: casc0:rel/nibling | surface: "nephew" | agent: {subj} | patient: {obj} | direction: agent-to-patient | gloss: {subj} is a nephew of {obj}.

gloss.intermediate_question: [query kinship/1] type: ? | agent: {subj} | patient: {obj} | direction: agent-to-patient | task: using only the records above, select the relation of {subj} (agent) to {obj} (patient).
gloss.final_question: [query kinship/1] type: ? | agent: {subj} | patient: {obj} | direction: agent-to-patient | task: using only the records above, select the relation of {subj} (agent) to {obj} (patient).
gloss.gloss_authoring_note: Explicator-authored raw-kernel Option-A records (ASM-1158 pre-freeze upgrade, 2026-07-11; authoring record + kernel-coverage mapping: poc/casc-0/kernel-coverage.md). Per-relation entries keyed by rel_surface (dict frames; the runner fail-closes ERR_FRAME on a missing key). Canonical head one field per claim: kind, type (casc0:rel/<core> — SELF-AUTHORED namespace, mapping to kernel-v0/molecules-v0 FAILED so the medium is kernel-STYLE, not kernel-covered), surface, agent, patient, direction (symmetric for sibling — the one relation whose direction field would otherwise mislead). Gloss clause varies by relation family on semantic grounds, never cosmetically: parent/grandparent glosses lead with the sex clause in adult terms (woman/man) then the core relation; child/grandchild glosses lead with the relation and mark sex age-neutrally (female/male); sibling glosses state the symmetric two-party fact; pibling/nibling glosses stay at the natural gendered surface (aunt/uncle/niece/nephew — English has no everyday neutral term) with no redundant sex clause. ANALYTIC-CONTENT BOUNDARY (content-matching discipline): every head field and gloss clause is a function of the single fact/claim triple (subj, surface term, obj) — core type, agent sex, direction/symmetry are lexical content of the surface word the content-matched nl sentence already carries; NO converse restatements, NO generation counts, NO decompositions (never 'an aunt is a parent's sister'), NO composition-table content, NO molecule explication bodies (birth semantics excluded — consistent with the kernel-STYLE rider).

prompt_frames.frames_note: CONTENT-MATCHED across mediums: same facts, same entities, same question, same 7-option gendered answer surface; per-medium token counts are MEASURED per cell (rho_in becomes MEASURED, converting the CASC/1 ESTIMATED ledger entry); NL phrasings produced by the same pinned template protocol as the dialect renderings so NL cells are not disadvantaged by authoring quality (cascade-synthesis section 3); gloss templates are EXPLICATOR-AUTHORED kernel-STYLE Option-A per-relation records (ASM-1142/1158 pre-freeze open item DISCHARGED 2026-07-11; per-relation dict frames keyed by rel_surface, runner fail-closes ERR_FRAME; authoring record + kernel-coverage mapping in poc/casc-0/kernel-coverage.md — mapping FAILED 0/7 core relation types, so the rider clause is kernel-STYLE, never kernel-covered); nl and plain templates untouched: the gloss additions (core type, agent sex, direction/symmetry) are analytic content of the surface word each nl sentence already carries, so information parity holds by construction

flop_accounting.formula: F0 section 3.3: FLOPs = 2*N_active*T_total + 2*L*T^2*d_attn per sequence; prefill and decode metered separately; T measured with the arm's own tokenizer
flop_accounting.cpu_note: CPU-side verifier/engine work metered as measured wall-clock seconds converted at this pinned rate ONLY for the aggregate figure; wall-clock reported separately
flop_accounting.usd_note: Modal list prices for planning + $-per-query conversion; actual billing reconciled from the dashboard

planning.note: dry-plan ESTIMATES only — never emitted as measurements

mock.note: SYNTHETIC mechanics-only stub LM (no torch); every number labelled MOCK, never a measurement; the engine, verifier (true AND deranged), eval-set construction, engagement accounting, TTC cost-matching, flop-meter and emitter code paths are the REAL ones. The planted skill surface (base + medium bonus + verifier bonus, larger at R2 than R3) exists ONLY so the analysis contract's gates and contrasts resolve during mock validation.

inputs_status.d-casc0: BUILT + pinned pre-freeze (data/d-casc0/build-dcasc0.py, generator seed dcasc0/1|casc-0|20260711, single-draw rule): 400 CLUTRR-shaped depth-2/3/4 relation-composition items with ENGINE-derivable gold, held-out compositions (eval depth-2 paths hash-disjoint from exemplar paths) and held-out depths (exemplars depth-2 only); the runner evaluates the deterministic rank prefix (per_arm_items=300)
inputs_status.kernel-v0 / molecules-v0: pinned verbatim from f2b-transfer for the gloss medium's authoring-style anchor; NOT read by the runner (the casc0 store is self-contained) — retained in the pin block so the record's medium claim is anchored to the same kernel instance the f2b line measured
